channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: WATCH: 2 window washers rescued from 'spinning' scaffolding in New York City
item.description: The wind took over a scaffolding while two workers were washing the windows of a high-rise building in Midtown.
item.dc:creator: Eliecer Marte, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FedEx cargo plane makes emergency landing following bird strike, engine fire
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — A FedEx cargo plane made an emergency landing at a busy New Jersey airport on Saturday after a bird strike caused an engine fire that could be seen in the morning sky.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Namibian founding president Sam Nujoma is laid to rest and praised as the last African liberator
item.description: WINDHOEK, Namibia (AP) — Namibia’s founding president, Sam Nujoma, who steered the country to freedom from apartheid South Africa, was laid to rest in the country’s Heroes Acre cemetery on Saturday following a state funeral attended by African leaders and foreign dignitaries.
item.dc:creator: SONJA SMITH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: WATCH: Flames from FedEx flight after bird strike near Newark Airport
item.description: NEWARK, New Jersey (PIX11) -- A FedEx flight was forced to make an emergency landing at Newark Liberty International Airport after a bird strike on Saturday morning, according to the FAA.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks Trump order threatening funding for institutions that offer care for transgender youth
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump’s plan to pull federal funding from institutions that provide gender-affirming care for transgender youth will remain blocked on a long-term basis under a federal judge's ruling in Seattle late Friday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Border czar makes the city of Boston his latest immigration target
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — As Mayor Michelle Wu prepares to go to Congress to defend Boston's protections of immigrant communities, the city is in a war of words with the Trump administration and Republican representatives over how much police should support deportations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Illinois jury announces verdict in killing of Palestinian American boy
item.description: A landlord from Illinois was found guilty of fatally stabbing a 6-year-old Palestinian American boy and critically wounding the boy's mother.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video shows implosion of iconic Six Flags rollercoaster Kingda Ka
item.description: The king has fallen. The legendary Kingda Ka ride at Six Flags Great Adventure in New Jersey was imploded on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WGN 9

item.title: Alabama governor commutes death row inmate Rocky Myers' sentence to life in prison
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey commuted the death sentence of Robin “Rocky” Myers to life in prison Friday, saying there were enough questions about his guilt that she could not move forward with his execution.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Peak bloom for the iconic cherry blossom trees of Washington, DC, is predicted for late March
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For decades, the thousands of blooming cherry blossom trees in the nation’s capital have served as both a magnet to visitors and the unofficial start of the tourist season for Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pennsylvania officer's last act after he was shot was to rescue a colleague, police chief says
item.description: A police officer who was shot to death while responding to an armed man with hostages inside a Pennsylvania hospital intensive care unit was remembered Friday as a dedicated public servant who did not hesitate to run toward danger and died a hero.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Actor Gene Hackman's death not from carbon monoxide, authorities say
item.description: Authorities investigating the deaths of legendary actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa say they have ruled out carbon monoxide poisoning as the cause.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Patrolling America's 'third border' from the skies
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard is taking to the skies to monitor Florida's coast as a crackdown on illegal immigration continues.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, are back in the US: What to know
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, arrived Thursday in the U.S. after authorities lifted travel restrictions on the siblings, who have millions of online followers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Wyoming's only full-service clinic stops providing abortions after new regulations signed
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyol (AP) — Wyoming's only full-service abortion clinic stopped providing abortions Friday, but it remained open after Gov. Mark Gordon approved a law requiring that such facilities be licensed as surgical centers.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Leonard Peltier remains defiant in AP interview, maintaining innocence and vowing continued activism
item.description: BELCOURT, N.D. (AP) — More than 50 years after a shootout on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation landed him in federal prison, Leonard Peltier remains defiant.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watch the full Trump-Zelenskyy Oval Office meeting
item.description: President Donald Trump shouted at Ukraine's leader on Friday during an extraordinary meeting in the Oval Office, berating President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for “gambling with millions of lives” and suggesting his actions could trigger World War III.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope Francis suffers medical setback, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered a bronchial spasm on Friday that resulted in him breathing in vomit, requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying a setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Where things stand as Congress tries to avoid a partial government shutdown in two weeks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As House members finished voting for the week and left Washington, the lead Democrat on the House Appropriations Committee, Rep.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Zelenskyy's White House meeting with Trump, Vance turns to shouting match
item.description: A White House meeting that was intended to kick off negotiations over a deal that would offer the United States access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals unraveled Friday afternoon as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy clashed with President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance in the Oval Of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and JUSTIN SPIKE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife tested negative for carbon monoxide, sheriff says
item.description: Actor Gene Hackman, his wife Betsy Arakawa, and their dog were found dead in their Santa Fe home, in a scene that one detective had deemed "suspicious enough in nature to require a thorough search and investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fallon Fischer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump, Vance go off on Zelensky in contentious Oval Office spat
item.description: President Trump went off on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky during an extraordinary Oval Office meeting in front of cameras on Friday in which he raised his voice and called the foreign leader “disrespectful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KTLA 5

item.title: Best mullet? Best use of Taylor Swift? AP hands out its own movie awards ahead of the Oscars
item.description: Hollywood's never-ending awards season can make it easy to get a little lost in the run-of-the-mill “best” categories. Actor. Actress. Director. Picture. A great movie can be reduced to a single performance; those that are left out seem to simply vanish for a while.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE and LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's transportation department cancels new review step that could've slowed state projects
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration is backing off its demand for an extra layer of federal scrutiny whenever states seek even minor changes to their transportation plans, after the rescinded requirement sparked concern that some payments for roads, bridges and transit would be de
item.dc:creator: JEFF McMURRAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Skype shutting down in May as Microsoft pivots to Teams as alternative
item.description: (KRON) -- Skype will be shutting down after two decades, the internet-based phone and video platform announced on Friday. Skype will be going dark in May with parent company Microsoft looking to driver users to Microsoft Teams as an alternative.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Those with gender dysphoria can no longer serve in military: Pentagon
item.description: (NewsNation) — A memo sent to Defense Department leaders Thursday states those with gender dysphoria are no longer eligible for military service. This document, written by Darin S.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Federal workers start to get a new email demanding their accomplishments
item.description: Federal employees are starting to receive a new email requiring them to explain their recent accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Once-dominant video conferencing service shutting down
item.description: Skype, a groundbreaking service in the world of internet-based communications, will shut down after more than two decades of operations, parent company Microsoft announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Washington man arrested for stealing $8M fishing boat with crew still on board, officials say
item.description: The suspect allegedly tried to steal a different boat earlier that morning, but its crew "ran him off the boat," police said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vice President JD Vance, a Catholic, acknowledges the pope's criticism of US immigration crackdown
item.description: Vice President JD Vance acknowledged Pope Francis’ criticism of the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigration, without responding to any of its specifics or to the pontiff’s apparent criticism of Vance’s own deployment of Catholic tradition to justify such policies.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal agencies plan second email asking workers for weekly accomplishments
item.description: Some agencies plan to send a second Saturday email to federal workers asking them for bullet points on what they did this week, a sign at least some departments plan to continue with the controversial practice ignited by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: To her, Hurricane Helene debris isn’t trash. It is full of memories
item.description: SWANNANOA, N.C. (AP) — The tops of dried, bent cornstalks crunch underfoot. Jill Holtz’s gaze is fixed on the ground ahead. She wanders into the nearby woods and weaves between twisted branches. Then, Holtz spots something and starts to riffle through the withered twigs.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4
